verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70-1/HSP72) is canonically a cytosolic stress-inducible chaperone with no TM domain, GPI anchor, or constitutive surface mechanism. However, multiple independent studies document its translocation to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane specifically on tumor cells and under stress conditions (heat, oxidative stress, oncogenic transformation), where it is detectable by flow cytometry and surface biotinylation. The mechanism involves insertion into lipid rafts/negatively-charged phospholipids (phosphatidylserine) in a non-classical, ER/Golgi-independent manner. This cancer-cell-restricted ecto-pool is the established surface story and represents a documented non-baseline surface biology qualifying as cell_state_induced. The baseline localization is cytoplasmic; surface display is restricted to stress/transformed states.
